stories.title: The next review exposed its boundaries.
stories.brief: Repository choices, activity since the last review, and scheduled scope became visible before collection, turning an invisible automation into an inspectable contract.
stories.verdict: A strong trust boundary for automation
stories.whatChanged: The reader gained live repository selection, a review cursor, bounded activity windows, and a preview of what the next scheduled review would inspect.
stories.whyItMatters: Automation is easier to trust when its inputs are legible. The reader can see which repositories are in scope and whether they contain candidate work before compilation begins.
stories.verify: Select and remove repositories, then confirm the preview distinguishes newly added, hidden scheduled, inaccessible, candidate, and quiet sources.
stories.tradeoff: The preview described scope but did not yet feed reader intent back into the compiler; that closed-loop handoff arrived in the following edition.
stories.evidence: 1 feature commit · bounded activity and visible scheduled scope

codeEvidence.title: Make the review cursor explicit and bounded
codeEvidence.why: The activity window accepts a saved review cursor only when it is valid and no more than 90 days old, otherwise falling back to a bounded rolling window.
